cc.advTip: Feed rates and spindle speed — defaults are usually fine
cc.ballTip: Ball-nose: best for smooth relief finishing
cc.bitLiveHint: feeds drive the safe-passes suggestion below.
cc.cdrUnsupported: CorelDRAW .cdr is a proprietary binary format with no reliable in-browser parser. In CorelDRAW choose File → Export → DXF (or SVG/EPS) and import that — karmyogi fully supports DXF, including splines & ellipses.
cc.closedTip: Closed loops — needed for Profile / Pocket
cc.connectFirst: Connect to a machine to send
cc.decimalsTip: Number of decimal places in emitted coordinates
cc.depthOverStock: Cut depth ({d}mm) is deeper than the stock ({h}mm) — the tool may hit the bed.
cc.dropHint: or drop a .stl / .step / .dxf / .eps / .ai file anywhere on this panel
cc.engraveHelp: Follow every line at one depth — good for V-carving text & detail.
cc.errEps: Couldn’t parse this EPS/AI — export as DXF.
cc.errNoTriangles: STL parsed but contained no triangles.
cc.exportStl: Export your STEP model as STL to carve it.
cc.finishing: Finishing — parallel raster following the relief surface
cc.importToGen: Import a model to generate a toolpath.
cc.intro: Import an STL to carve in relief, or a DXF / vector file for 2D engrave · profile · pocket — the toolpath previews live in the Visualizer, then press Send.
cc.maxDepthHint: Carve depth limits how far below the model’s top surface the tool goes (protects the bed / clamps).
cc.needClosed: {op} needs a closed contour — none found in this file.
cc.notConnected: Not connected — preview is live; connect to send.
cc.penHint: Pen: cuts map to pen-down Z, travels to pen-up Z (no spindle).
cc.penModeTip: Pen plotter: cuts → pen-down Z, travels → pen-up Z (no spindle)
cc.pocketHelp: Clear out the inside area of closed shapes, pass by pass.
cc.profileHelp: Cut along closed shapes (on / inside / outside the line).
cc.recIntro: Safe passes worked out for {mat} with a {bit} — let karmyogi decide for you.
cc.roughing: Roughing — clear bulk stock in flat stepdown layers
cc.sendTip: Stream this program to the machine
cc.spindleHint: Spindle: Z values are written verbatim; M3/M5 wrap the program.
cc.spindleModeTip: Router/spindle: Z is cut depth; M3/M5 control the spindle
cc.stepUnsupported: STEP import isn’t supported yet — full ISO-10303 (B-rep) parsing is out of scope. Please export your model as STL (most CAD tools: File → Export → STL) and import that instead.
cc.stepoverFracTip: Sideways overlap between pocket passes, as a fraction of tool ⌀
cc.stepoverHint3d: Smaller stepover = finer surface but more passes. Ball-nose is the classic relief finishing tool.
cc.stockLiveHint: The block shows live in the 3D Visualizer — look there to check fit.
cc.surfaceZTip: Z of the stock top — cuts go from here down to Cut depth

motion.alert.corruptPlural: {count} settings look corrupted. Review the highlighted rows below, or factory-reset to recover.
motion.alert.corruptSingular: 1 setting looks corrupted. Review the highlighted rows below, or factory-reset to recover.
motion.confirm.clearOffsets:
Clear all work-coordinate offsets (G54–G59, G28/G30, G92)?

Machine settings are kept; only your zero offsets are erased.
motion.confirm.resetSettings:
Restore the machine to its known-good default configuration?

This runs $RST=$ then writes the karmyogi default profile (steps/mm 1600, max rate 1000, accel 30, max travel 200, etc.) — use it to recover a corrupted or mis-configured controller.
motion.confirm.wipeAll:
FULL EEPROM WIPE: reset BOTH settings AND coordinate offsets to defaults?

This is the nuclear option — everything goes back to factory.

motion.factory.resetSettings.title: $RST=$ — restore settings ($0–$132) to defaults
motion.factory.wipeAll.label: Wipe all ($RST=*)
motion.factory.wipeAll.title: $RST=* — full EEPROM wipe (settings + offsets)

pr.detect.alarm: Alarm during homing — check switches, then Unlock ($X) and retry.
pr.detect.autoTip: $H homing cycle, then read $130–$132 into the bed size. Moves the machine.
pr.detect.confirmNoTip: Cancel — do not move the machine
pr.detect.confirmQ: This will home the machine — keep clear. Continue?
pr.detect.confirmYesTip: Send $H and learn the workspace size
pr.detect.hint: Home the machine on its limit switches, then learn the work-area size from GRBL’s configured max travel. This drives the 3D bed grid and bed-fit checks.
pr.detect.homing: Homing… keep clear of the machine.
pr.detect.needHoming: Auto-home needs homing enabled. Open Motion / GRBL settings (⚙) and set $22=1 (homing) and wire your limit switches first.
pr.detect.noSettings: GRBL settings not read yet — press Sync, then retry.
pr.detect.noTravel: No usable max travel ($130–$132). Set them in Motion settings first.
pr.detect.noTravelAfter: Homed, but no usable max travel ($130–$132) to learn. Set them in Motion settings.
pr.detect.provenance: From GRBL’s configured max travel ($130–$132) — accurate only if those are calibrated to the real machine.
pr.detect.skipped: Skipped {axes} — set to 0 or unknown. Calibrate max travel in Motion settings.
pr.detect.title: Auto-detect workspace
pr.detect.useTravelTip: Copy the configured max travel ($130–$132) into the bed size. Does NOT move the machine.

print.intro.post: arrange it on the bed, slice to G-code, then preview & stream it to a GRBL printer.
print.note.post: (inset perimeters + alternating 0/90° rectilinear infill) for hobby GRBL-based printers — not a production slicer (no supports, bridging, or adaptive layers). Always sanity-check the toolpath in the Visualizer before printing.

probe.adv.caution: Caution: hard limits ($21) need limit switches physically wired. Many switches are normally-closed — if a switch reads triggered while open in the lights above, turn ON limit-pins invert ($5). Soft limits ($20) only work after a successful homing cycle.
probe.adv.hardLimitsDesc: stop on a limit switch trigger (needs switches wired)
probe.adv.limitInvertDesc: invert limit inputs — set ON for NC (normally-closed) switches
probe.adv.softLimitsDesc: refuse moves past $130–$132 max travel (needs homing)
probe.adv.syncTip: $$ — re-read settings so the toggles reflect the machine
probe.adv.title: Advanced: limits & homing
probe.intro: Find Z=0 with a touch probe, and watch your limit / probe switches live. Advanced GRBL limit & homing settings are tucked below.
probe.switch.connectNote: Connect to a GRBL device to see live pin states.
probe.switch.hint: Press a switch by hand — it lights up here.
probe.switch.title: Live switch detection
probe.z.lastProbeTip: $# — dump coordinate systems incl. PRB (last probe result) to the console
probe.z.lastSent: Last probe sent — check the console / "Show last probe".
probe.z.maxDistSub: give up if no contact within this far
probe.z.noAlarmTip: G38.3 Z- F — probe toward the workpiece, but do NOT alarm if no contact is made.
probe.z.plateSub: thickness of the probe / touch plate
probe.z.probeTip: G38.2 Z- F — lower the tool until it touches the plate. Alarms if no contact within the max distance.
probe.z.safety1: lowers the tool — clip the probe clip to the tool and rest the plate on the workpiece first. 1) Place the plate. 2)
probe.z.safety5: so Z=0 sits at the plate surface.
probe.z.setZeroTip: G10 L20 P0 Z<thickness> — set work Z=0 at the plate surface. Run after a successful probe.
probe.z.speedSub: how fast to lower toward the workpiece
probe.z.title: Z-Probe
